Postnatal Care Around the World

Author

Amal Mathew

Published

April 15, 2026

Introduction

Every mother deserves care after giving birth. Yet across the world, millions of women go without postnatal care in the critical first 48 hours.

This report explores global patterns in postnatal care for mothers, using UNICEF data on the percentage of women aged 15-49 who received postnatal care within two days of giving birth.

Key questions we explore:

  • Which countries have the lowest coverage?
  • Which countries have improved the most?
  • What does the global trend look like?

The Data

Code 
import polars as pl
import pandas as pd
from plotnine import *

df = pl.read_csv("unicef_indicator_1.csv")

df_clean = df.select([
    pl.col("country"),
    pl.col("alpha_3_code"),
    pl.col("time_period").alias("year"),
    pl.col("obs_value").alias("postnatal_pct")
]).drop_nulls()

print(f"Countries: {df_clean['country'].n_unique()}")
print(f"Years: {df_clean['year'].min()} to {df_clean['year'].max()}")
Countries: 112
Years: 2008 to 2024

World Map

The map below shows the most recent postnatal care rate for each country. Red means low coverage, green means high coverage.

Code 
import geopandas as gpd

df_latest = (
    df_clean
    .sort("year", descending=True)
    .unique(subset=["country"], keep="first")
    .to_pandas()
)

world = gpd.read_file(
    "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvkelso/natural-earth-vector/master/geojson/ne_110m_admin_0_countries.geojson"
)

world_merged = world.merge(
    df_latest, left_on="ADM0_A3", right_on="alpha_3_code", how="left"
)

(
    ggplot(world_merged)
    + geom_map(aes(fill="postnatal_pct"))
    + scale_fill_gradient(low="#d73027", high="#1a9850", na_value="lightgrey", name="Coverage %")
    + theme_void()
    + theme(figure_size=(12, 6))
    + labs(title="Postnatal Care Coverage Worldwide")
)

Top and Bottom Countries

Code 
df_latest_pd = (
    df_clean
    .sort("year", descending=True)
    .unique(subset=["country"], keep="first")
    .to_pandas()
)

bottom10 = df_latest_pd.nsmallest(10, "postnatal_pct").copy()
top10 = df_latest_pd.nlargest(10, "postnatal_pct").copy()
bottom10["group"] = "Lowest"
top10["group"] = "Highest"

combined = pd.concat([bottom10, top10])

(
    ggplot(combined, aes(x="reorder(country, postnatal_pct)", y="postnatal_pct", fill="group"))
    + geom_col()
    + coord_flip()
    + scale_fill_manual(values={"Lowest": "#d73027", "Highest": "#1a9850"})
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(figure_size=(10, 7))
    + labs(title="Highest and Lowest Postnatal Care Countries", x="Country", y="Coverage (%)")
)

Global Trend Over Time

Code 
df_trend = (
    df_clean
    .group_by("year")
    .agg(pl.col("postnatal_pct").mean().alias("avg_pct"))
    .sort("year")
    .to_pandas()
)

(
    ggplot(df_trend, aes(x="year", y="avg_pct"))
    + geom_line(color="#2166ac", size=1.2)
    + geom_point(color="#2166ac", size=3)
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(figure_size=(10, 5))
    + labs(title="Average Postnatal Care Coverage Over Time", x="Year", y="Average Coverage (%)")
)

Coverage vs Year

Code 
df_pd = df_clean.to_pandas()

(
    ggplot(df_pd, aes(x="year", y="postnatal_pct"))
    + geom_point(alpha=0.4, color="#4393c3", size=2)
    + geom_smooth(method="lm", color="#d6604d")
    + theme_minimal()
    + theme(figure_size=(10, 6))
    + labs(title="Postnatal Care Rate vs Year", x="Year", y="Coverage (%)")
)

Conclusion

This data tells an important story:

  • Some countries like Turkmenistan and Belarus have nearly 100% coverage
  • Other countries like Chad and South Sudan are below 20%
  • The global trend is improving but millions of mothers still lack basic care

Data source: UNICEF Global Database - Postnatal Care for Mothers
